summary: Postinstall downloads and executes a binary over plaintext HTTP from a bare IP with no integrity check
details: The package's postinstall hook (`node install.js`) fetches a platform-specific native binary from `http://156.226.174.161:8888/binaries/tunnel-client-*` over plain HTTP, writes it to disk, chmods it 0755, and the package's CLI subsequently spawns it. The URL is unpinned (no version, no hash, no signature), the destination is a bare IP rather than a publisher-owned or known runtime CDN, and the transport is unencrypted. Any on-path attacker, or whoever controls 156.226.174.161, can deliver arbitrary native code to every installer's machine on `npm install`, with no tamper detection. This is the canonical install-time-rce dropper shape: lifecycle-script fetch + non-publisher infrastructure + no TLS + no integrity verification + immediate execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
